clinical_history: A 60-year-old woman with increasing exertional dyspnea and orthopnea is awaiting a cardiac surgical procedure. She gave a history of having a "heart murmur" and an "extra heart sound" first noted in her teenage years. Which ONE of the following is the likely cause of her problem? (Focus point clues: P waves, QRS axis, and R/S ratio in lead V1.)
diagnosis: Her ECG shows sinus rhythm with ventricular premature complexes. The relatively prominent R wave in V1 (by R/S ratio; not absolute R wave magnitude) in combination with right axis deviation is consistent with right ventricular hypertrophy (RVH). In addition, there is also prominent left atrial abnormality (LAA) evidenced by a biphasic P in V1 with a broad negative component and wide, notched P waves elsewhere). The ST-T changes inferiorly are non-specific. The combination of ECG findings consistent with RVH with marked left atrial abnormality (LAA) is strongly suggestive of mitral stenosis, usually due to rheumatic heart disease. The patient was pre-op for mitral valve replacement. Auscultation in the left lateral decubitus position revealed a diastolic "rumble," following an opening snap.

report.rhythm: Sinus rhythm with ventricular premature complexes
report.findings: Right ventricular hypertrophy (prominent R/S ratio in V1), left atrial abnormality (biphasic P in V1 with broad negative component; wide, notched P waves), non-specific inferior ST-T changes
report.impression: Mitral stenosis, likely due to rheumatic heart disease
